title: pseudopodospermum crispatulum
intro: Pseudopodospermum crispatulum is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, commonly known as the Himalayan or Indian Spurge. It is a perennial herb used in traditional medicine, valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Pseudopodospermum crispatulum include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin conditions, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Pseudopodospermum crispatulum can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat a variety of ailments in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
